context: Tests whether the solver keeps swift-language focused on core Swift type-system semantics and routes neighboring Codable, FormatStyle/localization, and API naming concerns to sibling skills.

checklist.name: Language owner
checklist.description: Says swift-language owns the core some/any/Never type-system decision.

checklist.name: Some guidance
checklist.description: Uses some for opaque returns, or simple generic-parameter shorthand when relevant, when one concrete type is preserved by the compiler; does not require covering both forms.

checklist.name: Any guidance
checklist.description: Uses any for heterogeneous storage, existential/type-erased values, dynamic dispatch, or runtime flexibility.

checklist.name: Never caveat
checklist.description: Explains that Never is uninhabited, is bottom-like in expression or inference contexts, and does not implicitly conform to arbitrary protocols; exhaustive type-theory taxonomy is not required.

checklist.name: Codable route
checklist.description: Routes full API response models, snake_case key strategy, and date decoding to swift-codable.

checklist.name: Format route
checklist.description: Routes detailed market-sensitive formatting to swift-formatstyle and mentions ios-localization for market, copy, or localized-display review.

checklist.name: Naming route
checklist.description: Routes public helper names and labels to swift-api-design-guidelines when that work appears; documentation-comment review is optional unless requested.

checklist.name: No sibling implementation
checklist.description: May include concise sibling-owner hints or brief API examples, but does not provide an end-to-end decoding pipeline, formatting matrix, localization plan, mini review checklist, or complete API naming rewrite.
